152		out := SyncResult{
153			Refs: make([]RefResult, 0, len(result.Plans)),
154			Counts: SyncCounts{
155				Applied: result.Pushed,
156				Skipped: result.Skipped,
157				Blocked: result.Blocked,
158				Deleted: result.Deleted,
159				Warned:  result.Warned,

168				BootstrapSuggested: result.BootstrapSuggested,
169				SourceHEAD:         result.SourceHEAD.String(),
170				Batch: BatchSummary{
171					Enabled: result.Batching,
172					Planned: result.PlannedBatchCount,
173					Done:    result.BatchCount,
174				},
175			},
176			Stats:       FromStats(result.Stats),
177			Measurement: FromMeasurement(result.Measurement),
178		}

180			out.Refs = append(out.Refs, RefResult{

183				TargetRef:  plan.TargetRef.String(),

197			copyItem := *item
198			out.Items[key] = &ServiceStats{
199				Name:          copyItem.Name,
200				Requests:      copyItem.Requests,
201				RequestBytes:  copyItem.RequestBytes,
202				ResponseBytes: copyItem.ResponseBytes,
203				Wants:         copyItem.Wants,
204				Haves:         copyItem.Haves,
205				Commands:      copyItem.Commands,
206			}
207		}
208		if len(stats.Sides) > 0 {
209			out.Sides = make([]SideBytes, 0, len(stats.Sides))
210			for _, side := range stats.Sides {
211				out.Sides = append(out.Sides, SideBytes{
212					Label:       side.Label,
213					Bytes:       side.Bytes,
214					Display:     side.Display,
215					ActiveNanos: side.ActiveNanos,
216					IdleNanos:   side.IdleNanos,
217				})
218			}
219		}
220		out.ElapsedNanos = stats.ElapsedNanos
221		return out
222	}
223	
224	func FromMeasurement(m syncer.Measurement) Measurement {
225		return Measurement{

227			ElapsedMillis:      m.ElapsedMillis,
228			PeakAllocBytes:     m.PeakAllocBytes,
229			PeakHeapInuseBytes: m.PeakHeapInuseBytes,
230			TotalAllocBytes:    m.TotalAllocBytes,
231			GCCount:            m.GCCount,
232		}
233	}

236		if hash.IsZero() {
237			return ""
238		}
239		return hash.String()

3	import "entire.io/entire/git-sync/internal/gitproto"

5	// ErrTargetRefMoved is returned (wrapped) by Sync and Replicate when a push was
6	// rejected because the target ref changed concurrently between this run's plan
7	// and its push — a benign, retryable compare-and-swap / lease miss, not a real
8	// failure. Test for it with errors.Is(err, gitsync.ErrTargetRefMoved).
9	//
10	// On the default push path the concrete error in the chain is a
11	// *RefRejectedError (reachable with errors.As). One case does NOT carry that
12	// concrete type: a BestEffort run with ForceWithLease escalates a lease miss
13	// through a plain wrapped error that still satisfies errors.Is(ErrTargetRefMoved)
14	// but is not a *RefRejectedError. So prefer errors.Is when you only need the
15	// cause, and treat a successful errors.As(*RefRejectedError) as best-effort.
16	//
17	// This is the supported way to distinguish a racing concurrent push from a
18	// genuine push failure; prefer it over inspecting the error message text, which
19	// is free-form and server-specific.
20	var ErrTargetRefMoved = gitproto.ErrTargetRefMoved
21	
22	// RefRejectedError is a single per-ref "ng" status returned by the target's
23	// receive-pack report-status, reachable with errors.As. Ref is the rejected ref
24	// and Reason is the raw server reason text. Rejections that are concurrent
25	// target-ref moves also satisfy errors.Is(err, ErrTargetRefMoved).
26	type RefRejectedError = gitproto.RefRejectedError

internal/internalbridge was a pure pass-through layer: it mirrored the
public request types field-for-field, wrapped syncer.Config in an opaque

convert every request through bridge mirror types, and unstable/client.go
round-tripped through them just to build a syncer.Endpoint literal.

Define the public result types (RefResult, Stats, ProbeResult, SyncResult,
...) directly in the root package instead of aliasing internal types --
this also makes their godoc visible on pkg.go.dev and exports SideBytes,
which was previously reachable through Stats.Sides but not nameable by
callers. Build syncer.Config directly in the client, matching what
unstable/client.go already does. Field sets and JSON tags are unchanged,
so the published API surface is identical.

98	// FetchCommitGraph fetches only the commit graph (tree:0 filter) for a ref.
99	// Requires v2 with filter support. Optional haves let the source skip commits
100	// already reachable from those hashes, which is valuable when planning later
101	// branches that share history with an already-planned trunk.
102	func (s *RefService) FetchCommitGraph(
103		ctx context.Context,
104		store storer.Storer,
105		conn Conn,
106		ref DesiredRef,
107		haves []plumbing.Hash,
108	) error {
109		if s.Protocol != "v2" {
110			return errors.New("commit graph fetch requires protocol v2")
111		}
112		if !s.V2Caps.FetchSupports("filter") {
113			return errors.New("source does not advertise fetch filter support")
114		}
115	
116		sortedHaves := SortedUniqueHashes(haves)
117		cmdArgs := make([]string, 0, 4+len(sortedHaves))
118		cmdArgs = append(cmdArgs,
119			"ofs-delta",
120			"no-progress",
121			"filter tree:0",
122			"want "+ref.SourceHash.String(),
123		)
124		for _, h := range sortedHaves {
125			cmdArgs = append(cmdArgs, "have "+h.String())
126		}
127		cmdArgs = append(cmdArgs, "done")
128	
129		body, err := EncodeCommand("fetch", s.V2Caps.RequestCapabilities(), cmdArgs)
130		if err != nil {
131			return err
132		}
133		reader, err := PostRPCStream(ctx, conn, transport.UploadPackService, body, true, "upload-pack fetch")
134		if err != nil {
135			return err
136		}
137		defer ioutil.CheckClose(reader, &err)
138		// Commit-graph fetches are short and not user-facing; skip progress.
139		return storeV2FetchPack(store, reader, false, nil)
140	}

143	// extracts (commit -> parent hashes) directly from the pack stream
144	// without materializing the full object set into a store. Peak memory
145	// during this call is dominated by the result map plus a small fixed
146	// delta-resolution cache; no commit content is retained.
147	//
148	// Same wire protocol as FetchCommitGraph (v2 fetch with tree:0 filter).
149	// Returns an empty map (not nil) when the source has no new commits.
150	func (s *RefService) FetchCommitParents(
151		ctx context.Context,
152		conn Conn,
153		ref DesiredRef,
154		haves []plumbing.Hash,
155	) (parents map[plumbing.Hash][]plumbing.Hash, err error) {
156		if s.Protocol != "v2" {
157			return nil, errors.New("commit parents fetch requires protocol v2")
158		}
159		if !s.V2Caps.FetchSupports("filter") {
160			return nil, errors.New("source does not advertise fetch filter support")
161		}
162	
163		sortedHaves := SortedUniqueHashes(haves)
164		cmdArgs := make([]string, 0, 4+len(sortedHaves))
165		cmdArgs = append(cmdArgs,
166			"ofs-delta",
167			"no-progress",
168			"filter tree:0",
169			"want "+ref.SourceHash.String(),
170		)
171		for _, h := range sortedHaves {
172			cmdArgs = append(cmdArgs, "have "+h.String())
173		}
174		cmdArgs = append(cmdArgs, "done")
175	
176		body, err := EncodeCommand("fetch", s.V2Caps.RequestCapabilities(), cmdArgs)
177		if err != nil {
178			return nil, err
179		}
180		reader, err := PostRPCStream(ctx, conn, transport.UploadPackService, body, true, "upload-pack fetch")
181		if err != nil {

191			}
192			parents = got
